extracted_items.content: The session evidence index and memory graph serve different purposes: evidence index is session-local search for raw tool outputs (ephemeral, FTS5-based), while memory graph is durable cross-session knowledge layer (LanceDB vectors + graph edges)

Phase 1 (Session Evidence Index) implementation uses SQLite FTS5 as per-session sidecar to index tool results (exec, read_file, write_file, web_fetch) with new session_search tool allowing agents to search back for specific code, errors, or outputs

User preference: implement the evidence index (Phase 1) because it enables agents to see what they may have already found via tool calls or messages from earlier, giving them powerful history search capability
User corrected assistant for launching Claude Code instead of Codex when user specifically requested Codex to work on the evidence index implementation
Session evidence index is session-local and disposable; content is raw command output, file contents, API responses; searchable via keyword/full-text search; serializes as single SQLite file

Memory graph is permanent durable knowledge layer for distilled facts, decisions, and preferences; cross-session and cross-agent; built on LanceDB vectors plus graph edges with semantic + keyword hybrid search

Goal: build a robust memory graph system that slowly accumulates lessons learned across all agent chats, making it an essential part allowing agents to find important context on what user is working on and trying to accomplish

Phase 1 (evidence index) is simpler and ships fast but does not contribute to cross-agent learning loop; Phase 2 (extraction decoupling + smarter routing) is higher strategic value for building durable knowledge graph
